div.head: The Consul at Bilbao (Chapman), Then in France, to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
St. Jean de Luz, July 4, 1937—1 p.m.
[Received July 4—11:15 a.m.]

div.p: Department’s telegram No. B–203, June 14, 2 p.m. Spanish Consul at Hendaye called July 2nd to discuss question of admission of 500 Basque children, all under 16, into the United States. He has since furnished a letter showing the children are still to be evacuated which doubtless means Basque children from Bilbao now at Santander. I have furnished Consul General outline legal requirements to be met reserving the right to determine each individual case upon its legal merits.
